model: ProofCast editorial (composed from TxLINE-verified events + web2 names)
text:
What a night in the World Cup! Quarter-final, 11 July 2026, Miami — and Billed as Haaland versus Kane, it was Jude Bellingham who decided it — and ended Norway's fairytale. Two of the tournament's most feared number nines shared the marquee, but the headlines belonged to someone else entirely.

36' — Andreas Schjelderup buried it, 1–0! 47' — Jude Bellingham lashed it home, 1–1! 93' — Jude Bellingham made no mistake, 1–2!

Andreas Schjelderup gave Norway a deserved lead late in the first half, and the dream of a first semi-final flickered. Bellingham levelled in first-half stoppage time with a driving run and a cool finish. He struck again in extra time to send England to their first semi-final since 2018 and bring Erling Haaland's remarkable run to a shocking end. Neither Haaland nor Kane found the net; the game's biggest stage found a different hero.

You could feel the tension crackle around the ground with every attack.

This is knockout football at its rawest — win, or fly home.

The neutrals were on their feet; nobody in the building sat still.

Golden Boot, glory, the lot — it was all on the line out there.

FULL-TIME: Norway 1–2 England! England march on, Norway head home heartbroken — and here's the beautiful part: every goal, every minute you just heard is locked on-chain and verified by TxLINE. No hot air, no guesswork, just receipts. What. A. Game!
